metadata.title:
IS IT POSSIBLE THAT RAILROADS


REALLY WANT A UNION DEPOT?

pages.raw_text:
Is it possible that the agitation
for a union depot is part of plan
that makes the public Of Spokane
appear as supplicant for union
passenger terminal, whereas the
railroads are the real movers in
the deal, and that the agitation has
been inaugurated to get the city
council to make lower price on
the city hall?


today made the statement that it
looks very much like some deal of
this kind was being engineered.
A prominent business man made
the statement at the same time
that the union station matter had
long been fixed up In Chicago by
the railroads interested and that
the campaign for a union depot on
the part of citizens was being en-
gineered in order to secure the
most valuable piece of land in
volved, that owned by the city at
Front avenue and Howard street.


The members of the city council
will meet the union depot commit
tee for conference as to the value
Of the ground this afternoon at the
city hall. The council has fixed the
price at 1852, 900, while 1t was
agreed by the railroad magnates
and the depot committee that
$276, 001 was Q big price. There is
considerable opposition in the city
council to lowering the price, and a
strong minority that believe the
price should be lowered
